records.ety: [F. rumb, Sp. rumbo, or Pg. rumbo, rumo, probably fr. Gr. <?/<?/<?/ a magic wheel, a whirling motion, hence applied to a point of the compass. See Rhomb.]

defs: A line which crosses successive meridians at a constant angle; -- called also rhumb line, and loxodromic curve. See Loxodromic.
